title: Bolanthus thessalus
intro: Bolanthus thessalus, also known as Greek tea, is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of Greece and Turkey, belonging to the Gentianaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Bolanthus thessalus include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Bolanthus thessalus is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
